input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on macOS:

---

## 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & tools |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload, or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
# After installing the .NET SDK:
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in everything: MAUI, Android SDK, Android emulator, iOS build support, and the JDK if missing.

## 3. Accept Licenses & Configure Xcode

```bash
# Accept Xcode license
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# Ensure Xcode path is set
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
```

## 4. Discover the Right Versions

- **Supported .NET version**: Check [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) — MAUI ships with each .NET release (e.g., .NET 8, .NET 9).
- **Xcode version**: Each MAUI release documents the required Xcode version in [platform dependencies](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation).
- **Android API levels**: The workload installs the recommended SDK. To check or add more:
  ```bash
  dotnet android sdk list
  dotnet android sdk install --package "platforms;android-35"
  ```

## 5. Verify the Installation

```bash
# Check SDK & workloads
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios

# Run on Android Emulator (create one first via Android SDK)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 6. Optional but Recommended

- **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight IDE experience on macOS.
- **Android Emulator**: Create a device via `dotnet android avd create` or use Android Studio's AVD Manager if installed.

## Key Gotchas

- **Xcode version mismatch** is the #1 issue — always verify the MAUI docs list your Xcode version as supported before upgrading either.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
- Physical iOS device deployment requires an Apple Developer account and provisioning profile.

Let me provide a comprehensive, concise plan.
